title: 7.06.2026 — Nika, Day 3
paragraphs: In the morning, Sasha woke me up with the words: 'We need to go up the mast and tighten the shrouds. Can you do it?' I replied: 'No problem.' And so we did — they hoisted me up by a halyard in a special bosun's chair, and I secured the shrouds. It's some kind of fate — in my entire sailing career, I've only been on 5 or 6 yachts, and on two of them I was hoisted up the mast — strung up on the yardarms, so to speak))) Then we had breakfast, took a walk... We kept glancing at the sea, and somehow it didn't promise a gentler reception than yesterday. Sasha was ready to stay for another night. I suggested: 'Why don't we head out? If it's no good, we'll turn around and stay the night? What's stopping us from trying a hundred times and turning back 99 of them?' Sasha agreed.

We set out around 17:00, and the sea welcomed us with love. I stood another night watch. Woke up in the morning, stepped out into the cockpit, and looked at the horizon... I grab the binoculars... thinking — am I still half-awake? I ask Krzysztof: 'What is that?' He takes the binoculars: 'A bridge?' Me: 'An unfinished one, some 300 meters long??? There are no bridges like that in the Gulf of Riga.' I call out to Sasha: 'Sasha, come up to the cockpit, please!' He: 'What happened?' Me: 'I see something and I don't understand what it is...' He: 'Inverted islands?' Me: 'Yes!' He: 'It's just a Fata Morgana.' Me: 'Just'??? You say 'just'??? And so the Universe and the Baltic Sea gifted me a fantastic spectacle)
